claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 14 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 14 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess this properly, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and any children you're responsible for.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children under 16 that you're responsible for, or are you responsible for any young people aged 16-19 in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have two children - Alex, who's 8, and Blake, who's 14. They both live with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great to know you have Alex (8) and Blake (14) living with you.

Before I can assess your eligibility, I need to check a few more details about your circumstances.

**Are you currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. That's helpful to know.

Now I need to understand your immigration or residency status, as this can affect eligibility.

**What is your current immigration or residency status in the UK? For example, are you a British citizen, do you have settled status, pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or something else?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. That's straightforward for eligibility purposes.

Now I need to check one more important detail about your circumstances.

**Are you the only person claiming or receiving Child Benefit for Alex and Blake, or is someone else also claiming for them?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm the only one - nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, that's clear. Now let me ask about your financial situation and work status, as this can be relevant to eligibility.

**Are you currently employed, self-employed, looking for work, or in some other situation (such as studying, retired, or caring for someone)?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

Just to clarify one more detail about your circumstances:

**Are you currently receiving any of the following benefits: Income Support, income-based Jobseeker's Allowance, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not receiving any of those benefits.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me confirm what you've told me:
- You have two children: Alex (8) and Blake (14)
- Both children live with you in the UK
- You are a British citizen
- You are the only person claiming for them
- You are employed
- You are not receiving Income Support, income-based Jobseeker's Allowance, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit

Based on the Child Benefit rules, you meet all the eligibility criteria. You are responsible for both children (they live with you), you live in the UK, you are a British citizen, and no one else is claiming for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Here's a summary of why you qualify:

✓ **For Alex (age 8):** You are responsible for Alex (they live with you), you live in the UK, you are a British citizen, and no one else is claiming for them.

✓ **For Blake (age 14):** You are responsible for Blake (they live with you), you live in the UK, you are a British citizen, and no one else is claiming for them. 

**Important note:** When Blake turns 16, you can continue to receive Child Benefit as long as Blake remains in approved education or training (such as school, college, or certain training programmes).

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit through the official government website. If you have any further questions about the application process, feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old and lives with you in the UK. As the person responsible for Alex (living with them) and a British citizen living in the UK with no one else claiming, you qualify for Child Benefit for Alex under the standard rules.

Blake is 14 years old and lives with you in the UK. As the person responsible for Blake (living with them) and a British citizen living in the UK with no one else claiming, you qualify for Child Benefit for Blake under the standard rules. Note: When Blake turns 16, you may continue to receive Child Benefit if Blake stays in approved education or training.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake. You meet all the key eligibility requirements: you are responsible for both children (they live with you), you live in the UK, you are a British citizen, and you are the only person claiming for them. Your employment status and benefits situation do not affect your eligibility. You can claim Child Benefit for both children."

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old and lives with you in the UK. As the person responsible for Alex (living with them) and a British citizen living in the UK with no one else claiming, you qualify for Child Benefit for Alex under the standard rules.

Blake is 14 years old and lives with you in the UK. As the person responsible for Blake (living with them) and a British citizen living in the UK with no one else claiming, you qualify for Child Benefit for Blake under the standard rules. Note: When Blake turns 16, you may continue to receive Child Benefit if Blake stays in approved education or training.

Your employment status and benefits situation do not affect your eligibility. You can claim Child Benefit for both children."
